response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack engineer with genuine strengths in distributed, high-throughput systems (millions of requests/sec at Sociomantic) and a strong software-craftsmanship and mentoring track record. However, the role's core minimum bar — 8 years of C++ — is not met; C++ appears only in a ~6-month stint at National Instruments and a university thesis. The primary languages are TypeScript/Node and DLang, not C++, and there is no production cloud-compute-platform architecture or formal technical-leadership-of-cloud-engineers experience. The gap against the hard minimum qualifications is too large for this specific position.
strengths: Low-latency distributed/microservices experience handling millions of requests per second
Strong engineering culture: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, mentoring, workshops
Broad full-stack and infrastructure exposure (Docker, Kubernetes, CI servers, DevOps)
concerns: C++ experience is roughly 6 months, far short of the 8-year minimum
No production cloud-compute-platform architecture or compute/storage/hardware focus
No formal technical-leadership role mentoring cloud or DevOps engineers

key_factors.factor: Lack of cloud-platform architecture and tech-lead role
